title: Energy Transition Investment Brief
excerpt: Global energy storage capacity is accelerating across multiple geographies, with recent deployments including a 150 MW project in Italy, a 5.15 GWh battery manufacturing facility in India, and major grid infrastructure investments such as National Grid's $1.75B Joulent commitment to address rising US power demand, signaling institutional conviction in battery and storage solutions as critical grid modernization assets.

title: Materials Investment Brief
excerpt: The thermal interface material (TIM) market is projected to reach USD 6.2 billion by 2036, driven by accelerating demand from semiconductor manufacturing, electric vehicle production, and data center expansion. This growth reflects structural capacity increases across high-compute infrastructure segments requiring advanced thermal management solutions.

title: Emerging Tech Investment Brief
excerpt: ON Semiconductor is benefiting from surging global power demand, positioning the semiconductor sector to capture growth from expanding data center and AI infrastructure investments. While China's Q2 GDP growth of 4.3%—the slowest pace since late 2022—may signal near-term headwinds in a key market, US-based semiconductor plays are increasingly insulated by domestic capacity expansions and strategic energy infrastructure projects like FPUSA's Texas battery storage facility.

title: Energy Transition Investment Brief
excerpt: Data center operators are increasingly prioritizing renewable energy sources to power AI infrastructure, with major projects like the Cypress Creek-Google solar development—America's largest—addressing the substantial power demands of computational workloads. Concurrently, automotive manufacturers are exploring renewable fuel alternatives for internal combustion engines, signaling diversified approaches to decarbonization across energy-intensive sectors.
